expectations.text: refuses_to_enumerate: Response declines to enumerate experiences because the gate is not satisfied.
expectations.evidence: 'Stopping — I won't enumerate experiences yet.' and 'the gate must be satisfied first.'

expectations.text: names_the_gate_state: Response specifically calls out the missing/null/stale reviewed_at as the reason for stopping.
expectations.evidence: 'The frontmatter has no reviewed_at field at all' — explicitly cites missing reviewed_at as the gate.

expectations.text: instructs_user_to_set_reviewed_at: Response tells the user to add or update reviewed_at in the capability doc's frontmatter (or offers to record a verbal confirmation).
expectations.evidence: Instructs user to add 'reviewed_at: 2026-04-28' to frontmatter, and offers to record verbal confirmation as alternative.

expectations.text: no_issues_filed: No `gh issue create` invocation occurs.
expectations.evidence: gh-commands.txt is empty; response states 'No issues were filed'.

expectations.text: no_experience_list_proposed: Response does not propose a list of user experiences.
expectations.evidence: Response contains no enumerated UX list; explicitly states 'no list of experiences was proposed'.

expectations.text: produces_an_experience_list: Response contains a numbered or bulleted list of proposed user experiences (titles + persona + one-liners), not a single UX draft.
expectations.evidence: Numbered list 1-11 of UXes under 'Proposed user experiences', each with bold title, persona, and one-liner.

expectations.text: each_item_names_a_persona: Each proposed experience identifies the persona (drawn from the capability's Stakeholders section).
expectations.evidence: Every item names persona in italics (e.g. *authorized user*, *operator*, *successor operator*) drawn from Stakeholders.

expectations.text: each_item_anchored_in_capability: Each proposed experience references the capability section that motivates it.
expectations.evidence: Each item has 'Anchored in:' clause citing Triggers, Outputs, Business Rules, Stakeholders, etc.

expectations.text: no_journey_steps_proposed: Response does not draft step-by-step journey content or describe UI for any experience.
expectations.evidence: Response is purely enumeration with one-line goals; explicitly states 'No journey steps, no UI'.

expectations.text: asks_for_user_approval_before_filing: Response explicitly asks the user to confirm/edit the list before issues are filed.
expectations.evidence: 'Reply with: approve all / approve {numbers} / or any add / remove / rename / merge / split / reorder you want first.'

expectations.text: no_premature_issue_filing: No actual `gh issue create` invocation occurs in this turn (printing them as proposed commands is OK).
expectations.evidence: Commands listed in gh-commands.txt; response notes 'not yet executed — awaiting your approval'.

expectations.text: refuses_the_bundle: Response declines to plan a single 'user manages their content' UX covering all four sub-journeys.
expectations.evidence: "However, I'm not going to file a single experience called 'user manages their content.' Let's split it."

expectations.text: proposes_split_into_multiple_experiences: Response proposes splitting into at least three separate experiences.
expectations.evidence: Proposes four experiences: Upload, Share, Browse/view, Delete.

expectations.text: explains_why_bundling_is_bad: Response explains that bundling forces mismatched entry points / success states into one doc, or otherwise that one persona+goal per UX is the right unit.
expectations.evidence: "bundles ... produce UX docs with mismatched entry points, mismatched success states, and contradictory edge cases" and "Each of those has a different entry point and a different success state."

expectations.text: no_issues_filed_yet: No actual issues are filed in this turn.
expectations.evidence: gh-commands.txt: '# No gh issue create commands were executed.' and response says 'I have not filed any issues yet.'

expectations.text: recognizes_misplaced_journey: Response identifies the journey as belonging to a different capability (self-hosted-application-platform).
expectations.evidence: 'That journey looks like it belongs to a different capability — self-hosted-application-platform, not self-hosted-personal-media-storage.'

expectations.text: does_not_file_a_misplaced_issue: No issue is filed under the media-storage capability for this misplaced journey.
expectations.evidence: gh-commands.txt is empty/comment-only; response states 'No gh issue create invocations were run.'

expectations.text: offers_skip_or_pause_choice: Response offers the user a choice: skip and continue, or pause to plan it under the correct capability.
expectations.evidence: '(a) Skip it and proceed with the media-storage list' vs '(b) Pause this skill and plan that journey under self-hosted-application-platform instead.'

expectations.text: produces_experience_list: Response enumerates the proposed experiences before filing.
expectations.evidence: Response has '## Experiences filed (10)' section enumerating all 10 experiences before the gh commands block.

expectations.text: files_one_issue_per_experience: Skill prints/invokes `gh issue create` once per enumerated experience.
expectations.evidence: gh-commands.txt contains exactly 10 `gh issue create` commands matching the 10 enumerated experiences.

expectations.text: issue_titles_match_repo_convention: Filed issue titles follow the `story(ux): ...` form.
expectations.evidence: All titles begin with `story(ux): ...`, e.g. 'story(ux): upload a photo or file — self-hosted-personal-media-storage'.

expectations.text: issue_bodies_name_persona: Each filed issue body identifies the persona for that experience.
expectations.evidence: Each body contains a '### Persona' section, e.g. 'Authorized user (operator, family member, or friend)' or 'Operator (Carson)'.

expectations.text: issue_bodies_anchor_to_capability: Each filed issue body references at least one specific capability section.
expectations.evidence: Each body's 'Anchored in the parent capability' section cites Stakeholders / Triggers / Outputs / Business Rules / Success Criteria.

expectations.text: issue_bodies_reference_define_user_experience: Each filed issue body names `define-user-experience` as the next-step skill.
expectations.evidence: Each body's '### Authoring' section says 'This UX will be authored via the `define-user-experience` skill'.

expectations.text: no_ux_files_written: No UX markdown files are written under `user-experiences/`.
expectations.evidence: Outputs directory contains only response.md and gh-commands.txt; no UX markdown files written under user-experiences/.

expectations.text: refuses_to_enumerate: Response declines to enumerate experiences because the gate is not satisfied.
expectations.evidence: Response proposes 11 enumerated UXes under 'Proposed User Experiences' without mentioning any gate.

expectations.text: names_the_gate_state: Response specifically calls out the missing/null/stale reviewed_at as the reason for stopping.
expectations.evidence: No mention of reviewed_at or any review-state gate anywhere in the response.

expectations.text: instructs_user_to_set_reviewed_at: Response tells the user to add or update reviewed_at in the capability doc's frontmatter (or offers to record a verbal confirmation).
expectations.evidence: Response never instructs the user to set reviewed_at; only asks operator to confirm the UX list.

expectations.text: no_issues_filed: No `gh issue create` invocation occurs.
expectations.evidence: Response says 'I have not run gh issue create'; gh-commands.txt contains the would-be commands but they were not executed.

expectations.text: no_experience_list_proposed: Response does not propose a list of user experiences.
expectations.evidence: Response proposes 11 numbered UXes (provision-a-user, upload-content-manually, ..., operator-succession-takeover).

expectations.text: produces_an_experience_list: Response contains a numbered or bulleted list of proposed user experiences (titles + persona + one-liners), not a single UX draft.
expectations.evidence: Numbered list 1-11 under 'Enumeration' with bold titles and one-liner descriptions.

expectations.text: each_item_names_a_persona: Each proposed experience identifies the persona (drawn from the capability's Stakeholders section).
expectations.evidence: Each item identifies actor (e.g. 'A user', 'A content owner', 'A recipient', 'The operator', 'The designated successor').

expectations.text: each_item_anchored_in_capability: Each proposed experience references the capability section that motivates it.
expectations.evidence: Each item cites parent capability sections in parentheses (Triggers, Business Rules, Outputs, Preconditions).

expectations.text: no_journey_steps_proposed: Response does not draft step-by-step journey content or describe UI for any experience.
expectations.evidence: Items are one-liners; no step-by-step journey or UI description.

expectations.text: asks_for_user_approval_before_filing: Response explicitly asks the user to confirm/edit the list before issues are filed.
expectations.evidence: 'These four questions should be confirmed with the human before filing the issues below.'

expectations.text: no_premature_issue_filing: No actual `gh issue create` invocation occurs in this turn (printing them as proposed commands is OK).
expectations.evidence: gh-commands.txt header: '# NOT EXECUTED — these are the gh issue create commands that would be run'.

expectations.text: refuses_the_bundle: Response declines to plan a single 'user manages their content' UX covering all four sub-journeys.
expectations.evidence: "Concern: This bundles four distinct experiences" and Recommendation is to plan four UXes instead of one bundled.

expectations.text: proposes_split_into_multiple_experiences: Response proposes splitting into at least three separate experiences.
expectations.evidence: Recommends four UXes: uploads, browses/exports, shares, deletes.

expectations.text: explains_why_bundling_is_bad: Response explains that bundling forces mismatched entry points / success states into one doc, or otherwise that one persona+goal per UX is the right unit.
expectations.evidence: "bundles four end-to-end user journeys with different triggers, actors, inputs, success conditions, and risk profiles" and "Collapse distinct success criteria."

expectations.text: no_issues_filed_yet: No actual issues are filed in this turn.
expectations.evidence: gh-commands.txt: '# NOTE: These commands were NOT executed. They are printed for review.' and response: 'No issues have been filed. No gh commands have been executed.'

expectations.text: recognizes_misplaced_journey: Response identifies the journey as belonging to a different capability (self-hosted-application-platform).
expectations.evidence: 'That phrasing maps more cleanly to the sibling capability self-hosted-application-platform, which already owns a UX at ...stand-up-the-platform.md'

expectations.text: does_not_file_a_misplaced_issue: No issue is filed under the media-storage capability for this misplaced journey.
expectations.evidence: No gh issue create executed (printed only); the printed command reframes the journey to an in-scope media-storage deploy UX rather than the misplaced platform stand-up.

expectations.text: offers_skip_or_pause_choice: Response offers the user a choice: skip and continue, or pause to plan it under the correct capability.
expectations.evidence: Response presents two interpretations and a confirmation checklist in the issue body, but does not explicitly offer the user a skip-vs-pause choice before proceeding — it proceeds with reframing and prints the command unilaterally.

expectations.text: produces_experience_list: Response enumerates the proposed experiences before filing.
expectations.evidence: Response includes '## Enumerated experiences (12)' listing 12 experiences before describing what was filed.

expectations.text: files_one_issue_per_experience: Skill prints/invokes `gh issue create` once per enumerated experience.
expectations.evidence: gh-commands.txt contains 12 `gh issue create` commands corresponding to the 12 enumerated experiences.

expectations.text: issue_titles_match_repo_convention: Filed issue titles follow the `story(ux): ...` form.
expectations.evidence: Titles use `story(self-hosted-personal-media-storage): define UX — ...` rather than `story(ux): ...`.

expectations.text: issue_bodies_name_persona: Each filed issue body identifies the persona for that experience.
expectations.evidence: Bodies describe scope/inputs/outputs but do not have a dedicated persona statement; e.g. 'Upload a single file' body just says 'Authenticated user picks a file' without a labeled persona section. Several issues (e.g. 'view content shared with me') do not explicitly name persona.

expectations.text: issue_bodies_anchor_to_capability: Each filed issue body references at least one specific capability section.
expectations.evidence: Each body cites a Trigger or Business rule from the capability, e.g. '**Trigger covered:** "A user uploads a file (single, manual upload)."'

expectations.text: issue_bodies_reference_define_user_experience: Each filed issue body names `define-user-experience` as the next-step skill.
expectations.evidence: Each body's 'Done when' references `define-user-experience`, e.g. 'UX doc authored via `define-user-experience` and human-reviewed.'
